scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Source of truth: the official Chargebee OpenAPI spec (chargebee_api_v2_pc_v2_spec.json, https://github.com/chargebee/openapi, info.version 2026-06-12), which enumerates every endpoint on Chargebee's Product Catalog 2.0 API surface (427 operations across ~120 resource paths) plus the legacy Product Catalog 1.0 /plans list endpoint kept from the pre-Pass-B bundle for parity (retired from the current public OpenAPI spec but still live on Chargebee sites that have not migrated to Product Catalog 2.0). Every endpoint below is covered_by a stream/write action XOR excluded with a real category and reason (no blanket out_of_scope bucket): 32 GET list/detail endpoints are implemented as streams, 36 POST/DELETE mutations as write actions. Exclusions favor breadth of real business resources over: binary/PDF/e-invoice generation and async bulk export/file-upload jobs (binary_payload); site/account configuration, currency, custom-field, and migration metadata snapshots (non_data_endpoint); add-on/beta-gated surfaces such as omnichannel/app-store billing, Product-Catalog-2.0 migration tooling, multi-business-entity transfers, and metered grant-blocks (requires_elevated_scope); a resource explicitly marked obsoleted by Chargebee's own docs, or a short-lived tokenization artifact fully superseded once redeemed into a durable record (deprecated / duplicate_of); irreversible hard-deletes and sandbox-only clock control with a safer, already-covered alternative (destructive_admin); and compound multi-step checkout/quote/estimate workflows, subscription/invoice/credit-note operational side-actions (dunning, proration, refund application), and narrow catalog/packaging sub-resource management beyond the core CRUD triads (out_of_scope, each with its own specific reason, not a shared boilerplate string).
